summary: Correctness-preserving refactoring that narrows CompanyEntityLookup boundaries by delegating inventory and purchasing lookups to focused company-scoped services. The change maintains all security invariants (wrong-tenant not-found, missing-id not-found, inactive-catalog-item rejection) while reducing coupling.

findings.description: CompanyEntityLookup now delegates to CompanyScopedInventoryLookupService and CompanyScopedPurchasingLookupService instead of directly accessing repositories
findings.recommendation: No action needed - this is the intended design improvement

findings.description: Preserved wrong-tenant/missing-id not-found semantics through delegation to focused services
findings.recommendation: No action needed - security boundary preserved

findings.description: Preserved inactive catalog-item rejection behavior through assertLinkedProductActive guard in new CompanyScopedInventoryLookupService
findings.recommendation: No action needed - business rule maintained

findings.description: Company-scoped repository queries remain the enforcement mechanism for tenant isolation
findings.recommendation: No action needed - tenant isolation preserved

findings.description: No new security boundaries introduced; existing boundaries maintained through delegation
findings.evidence: All touched services continue to filter by Company parameter before repository access

findings.description: Lock methods (lockActiveRawMaterial, lockActiveFinishedGood) preserved pessimistic locking semantics
findings.recommendation: No action needed - concurrency safety maintained

findings.description: New CompanyScopedInventoryLookupServiceTest provides 9 direct characterization tests covering all public methods
12 existing test classes updated to inject focused lookup services
findings.evidence: Handoff artifact lists updated test files with 136 tests passing in targeted packet suite

findings.description: CompanyEntityLookupTest simplified to focus on delegation verification
findings.evidence: CompanyEntityLookupTest.java reduced from comprehensive repository tests to delegation tests

findings.description: No shared mutable state introduced; all services remain stateless Spring singletons
findings.evidence: All touched service classes use constructor injection without mutable fields

findings.description: No circular dependency risk; dependency graph flows from CompanyEntityLookup -> focused services -> repositories
findings.evidence: CompanyScopedInventoryLookupService and CompanyScopedPurchasingLookupService have no dependency on CompanyEntityLookup

findings.description: No cross-module shared state concerns; inventory and purchasing services remain within their module boundaries
findings.evidence: CompanyScopedInventoryLookupService in inventory module, CompanyScopedPurchasingLookupService in purchasing module

findings.description: All existing callers updated to use new focused services directly where appropriate
findings.evidence: GoodsReceiptService, PurchaseInvoiceEngine, PurchaseOrderService, etc. updated to inject CompanyScopedInventoryLookupService and CompanyScopedPurchasingLookupService

findings.description: CompanyEntityLookup maintains backward-compatible API by delegating to focused services
findings.evidence: CompanyEntityLookup.java public methods unchanged, implementation delegates to new services
findings.recommendation: No action needed - API compatibility preserved

recommendations.description: Consider adding integration tests that verify end-to-end company isolation across the new service boundaries
recommendations.rationale: While unit tests cover individual services, integration tests would provide additional confidence in the complete flow

verificationCommands: cd erp-domain && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -q clean -DskipTests test-compile
